expected_findings.summary: 2 1099-B lot(s) missing Code W on Form 8949 (disallowed loss $3,030)
expected_findings.detail: The broker reported a wash sale loss disallowed amount on these lots, but Form 8949 column (f) does not carry Code W and column (g) does not add the disallowance back. IRC §1091 requires the disallowed loss to be tracked and added to the replacement-lot basis. This is a mechanical compliance error that routinely triggers IRS CP2000 notices because the IRS wash-sale computer match sees the broker-reported amount and expects it on the return.

expected_findings.summary: 1 RSU vest lot(s) with basis near $0 — likely double-counted
expected_findings.detail: The W-2 has a Box 12 code V (RSU / NQSO income) and the 1099-B has same-day sale-to-cover lots with a cost basis near zero. RSU vest income is already included in W-2 Box 1 wages; the 1099-B lot cost basis should equal the Box 1 inclusion amount, not zero. Leaving the basis at zero double-counts the vest as both wage income and a capital gain. This is the single most common mechanically detectable error in the public TurboTax record — a $3,200 refund delta is in Intuit's own public record.

expected_findings.summary: Casey has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Casey is 41 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: Quinn has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Quinn is 41 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.
Casey has contributed $14,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $9,500 can still be deferred before year end.
Quinn has contributed $6,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $17,500 can still be deferred before year end.

expected_findings.summary: Need $17,642 withheld to hit 90% current year safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: The alternative safe harbor is 90% of current year tax. This is usually only easier to hit than 110% prior year if income dropped.
